title: epipactis repens
intro: Epipactis repens, also known as Helleborine or Marsh Helleborine, is a rare and endangered orchid species native to Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Epipactis repens is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing blood pressure and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antiviral properties. Herbal preparations of Epipactis repens are typically made from the rhizomes and leaves, and can be consumed as teas, tinctures, or infusions.
